sprint_meta.notes: Agent 09 — Art/VFX volumetric primitive spec (Round 1, no code).

round_meta.build_target: Specs & contracts only — implementation in later rounds

wk19_r1_kickoff.prompt_text: Define visual volumetric mapping using Ursina built-in primitives; sizes for ~45° perspective; acceptance criteria and risks.

response.raw: Art direction for WK19 R1: map sim entities to simple 3D primitives with Y-up volumes on the X/Z floor plane (contract owned by Agent 03). Use Ursina stock models only for this MVP: cube for structures and most units, cylinder for heroes (capsule-like read) unless Ursina exposes a first-class capsule in our version—in that case prefer capsule for heroes. Scale in world units assuming 1 unit ≈ 1 tile on the ground (matches existing SCALE=32 px→world in ursina_renderer). Building footprint in X/Z must derive from BUILDING_SIZES × tile in world units (e.g. castle 3×3). Vertical extents: castle tallest (~2.0–2.5 units), 2×2 buildings mid (~1.2–1.6), lair slightly squat (~1.0), heroes ~1.0 height with ~0.25–0.3 radius, enemies red cubes ~0.5 on a side, peasants smallest. Class colors unchanged from current MVP (azure + ranger/wizard/rogue tints). Ground contact: entity origin at vertical center requires position.y = half_height + epsilon above floor. 45° pitch favors slightly taller than wide heroes and chunky enemies for silhouette.
summary_bullets: Coordinate system: floor = X/Z, vertical = Y (Ursina default); all footprint sizes tie to Agent 03’s px→(x,z) mapping and BUILDING_SIZES in tiles.
Structures: `cube` only — gold for castle, light_gray for generic buildings, brown for lairs; X/Z scale = footprint in world units, Y = tiered height (castle > 3×3 temples/fairgrounds > 2×2 > 1×1).
Heroes: `cylinder` (8–12 height segments) as the capsule stand-in — class tint on color; diameter ~0.5–0.6 world units, height ~1.0 for readable silhouette at ~45°.
Peasants / guards: `cube`, orange / yellow, smaller than enemies (peasant ~0.3, guard ~0.5) with guard slightly taller on Y if needed.
If Ursina version provides `Capsule` or equivalent one-liner, heroes may switch from cylinder to capsule without changing the size intent.
Keep unlit/flat appearance until lighting is explicitly in scope; avoid materials that fight the Pygame UI overlay contrast.
proposed_changes: Document a single lookup table (entity kind → model name, base color, footprint scale X/Z, height Y) for UrsinaRenderer once Agent 03’s contract is frozen.
Replace `_get_or_create_entity` default `quad`/`circle` with primitive-specific creation paths; per-type height and pivot rules (center-origin primitives sit half-height above ground).
Optional later: subtle class variant (e.g. ranger = slightly thinner cylinder) — out of scope for R1 contract text only.
acceptance_criteria: From a perspective camera pitched ~45–60°, player can distinguish heroes (tall cylinder/capsule), enemies (red cubes), castle (large gold cube), and generic buildings (gray prisms) without labels.
Footprint of every building primitive matches sim tile footprint in world X/Z (no shrinking castles vs guild halls when both are 3×3 or 2×2 per BUILDING_SIZES).
Vertical hierarchy reads: castle (and other 3×3 landmarks) ≥ smaller multi-tile buildings > units > peasants.
Class color language from current Ursina MVP is preserved (azure hero + ranger/wizard/rogue accents).
No entity intersects the conceptual ground plane (no sinking/over-floating beyond one small epsilon); minimal Z-fighting between overlapping units at same tile (acceptable MVP; note if stacks occur).
All primitives remain Ursina built-ins (cube, cylinder, sphere if needed for prototyping) — no custom meshes in WK19 R1 spec.
risks: Axis confusion: Ursina `scale` on cubes/cylinders applies per-axis; wrong axis mapped to height breaks the 45° read — must follow Agent 03’s Y=up contract strictly.
Cylinder default orientation may align height on wrong axis — may require rotation=(90,0,0) or equivalent so height is world Y; verify once in engine.
Perspective foreshortening: footprints measured on X/Z may look smaller at map edges; camera framing is Agent 03/08 — art sizes assume centered-ish view.
Replacing quads with 3D volumes increases overlap clutter in dense fights; readability may need slight unit scale reduction vs current flat MVP.
If dynamic lighting is enabled by default in perspective mode, flat colors may change — risk to HUD legibility; prefer unlit or consistent ambient until specified.
Lairs detected by `stash_gold` in current code — art must still read as distinct (brown, lower profile) without sim changes.
dependencies: Agent 03: frozen UrsinaRenderer contract for px → (world_x, world_z) and entity origin rules (center vs feet).
Agent 08: confirmation that camera.ui overlay and input mapping remain valid under perspective (parallel track).
Ursina/Panda3D version in use: confirms which primitive names and optional shapes (e.g. Capsule) are available.
questions_back_to_pm: Confirm target pitch angle band (plan says ~45–60°) — art proportions are tuned for mid-range (~50°).
Should ballista_tower (1×1) get a distinct primitive variant or same cube family as other 2×2 buildings scaled down?
recommended_next_actions: Agent 03 publishes the coordinate contract; Agent 09 reviews cylinder orientation with one spike screenshot in Ursina.
Round 2: implement primitive table in UrsinaRenderer only (per plan isolation), no sim changes.

volumetric_mapping_table.units: world units; 1 unit = 1 tile on X/Z unless Agent 03 revises SCALE
